DescriptionFRE: Les 26 et 27 août, l'équipe régionale de Health Care Without Harm en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes a organisé l'événement Vers la COP30 : Action climatique pour la santé en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes dans le cadre de sa stratégie pour la COP30. L'OPS a participé avec une intervention de Daniel Buss, responsable de l'Unité Changements climatiques et Déterminants environnementaux de la santé à l'OPS, présentant le travail de l'OPS sur l'action climatique, les ressources et les attentes formulées dans le Plan d'action pour la santé de Belém.
DescriptionPOR: Nos dias 26 e 27 de agosto, a equipe regional da Health Care Without Harm na América Latina e no Caribe realizou o evento Rumo à COP30: Ação Climática para a Saúde na América Latina e no Caribe como parte de sua estratégia para a COP30. A OPAS participou com uma intervenção de Daniel Buss, Chefe da Unidade de Mudanças Climáticas e Determinantes Ambientais da Saúde da OPAS, apresentando o trabalho da OPAS em ação climática, recursos e expectativas enquadradas no Plano de Ação em Saúde de Belém.

Title: NSA-2025-31-WPA_2
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 07: Health workforce;OCM 18: Social and environmental determinants;OCM 23: Health emergencies preparedness and risk reduction
HealthAgenda: Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services;Goal 3: Human Resources for Health;Goal 7: Evidence and Knowledge in Health;Goal 8: Outbreaks, Emergencies and Disasters
ProgressReport: Fernando Rementeria has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 11:33:04 AM: The Supporting Document synthesizes evidence-based recommendations drawn from PAHO’s Smart Hospitals initiatives, primarily implemented in health facilities in the Caribbean, as well as additional recommendations based on global south experiences from the Global Green and Healthy Hospitals network, coordinated by Health Care Without Harm, and the HCWH´s Health Care Climate Learning Initiative to showcase successful climate action case studies in health facilities. The document highlights how mitigation actions can also generate adaptation benefits, advancing the concept of climate resilience in health facilities as an integrated vision to face the climate crisis through hospital infrastructure. As part of the HCWH Strategy towards COP30, the document will contribute by consolidating regional lessons and showcasing examples that can be replicated in other contexts. These results are expected to inform policy dialogues and support the implementation of the Belém Action Plan.

DescriptionENG: As part of the Brazilian COP Presidency’s initiative during the Global Conference on Climate and Health in Brasilia, a Supporting Document was created in collaboration with PAHO under the Innovation and Production pillar of the Belém Health Action Plan. The initiative aimed to identify consolidated policies and best practices to guide countries in adapting to climate change and improving health outcomes.
DescriptionSPA: Como parte del llamado de la Presidencia brasileña de la COP en la Conferencia Global sobre Clima y Salud en Brasilia, se desarrolló un Documento de Apoyo en colaboración con la OPS bajo el pilar de Innovación y Producción del Plan de Acción de Salud de Belém. El llamado tenía como objetivo identificar políticas consolidadas y buenas prácticas para guiar a los países con opciones para adaptarse al cambio climático y mejorar los resultados en salud.

DescriptionFRE: Dans le cadre de l'appel de la présidence brésilienne de la COP lors de la Conférence mondiale sur le climat et la santé à Brasilia, un document de soutien a été élaboré en collaboration avec l'OPS sous le pilier Innovation et Production du Plan d'action pour la santé de Belém. L'appel visait à identifier des politiques consolidées et de bonnes pratiques pour guider les pays dans leurs options d'adaptation au changement climatique et d'amélioration des résultats en matière de santé.
DescriptionPOR: Como parte do apelo da Presidência Brasileira da COP durante a Conferência Global sobre Clima e Saúde em Brasília, um Documento de Apoio foi desenvolvido em colaboração com a OPAS sob o pilar de Inovação e Produção do Plano de Ação em Saúde de Belém. O apelo teve como objetivo identificar políticas consolidadas e boas práticas para orientar os países com opções para se adaptar às mudanças climáticas e melhorar os resultados de saúde.

Title: NSA-2025-31-WPA_3
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 07: Health workforce;OCM 18: Social and environmental determinants;OCM 23: Health emergencies preparedness and risk reduction

ProgressReport: Fernando Rementeria has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 11:33:04 AM: Delivery of a statement highlighting the need to advance both mitigation and adaptation in the health sector, building on the Belém Action Plan on Health, and leveraging ATACH to reinforce regional commitments. The participation emphasized the urgency of mobilizing more climate financing for the region and the opportunity to expand and adapt the Climate Smart Hospitals initiative. It showed how PAHO, working with NGOs such as Health Care Without Harm, can boost climate action in the health sector. Health Care Without Harm presented success stories, including carbon footprint calculations in Colombia, Peru, and Ecuador, developed in coordination with their ministries of health, as concrete examples of progress and opportunities for replication.

DescriptionENG: From September 29 to October 3, Health Care Without Harm participated in the 62nd Directing Council of the Pan American Health Organization. This participation emphasized the importance of embedding climate action into health sector priorities across the Americas, sharing lessons from regional initiatives, and fostering collaboration with ministries of health and PAHO to accelerate resilient, low-carbon health systems.
DescriptionSPA: Del 29 de septiembre al 3 de octubre, Health Care Without Harm participó en el 62º Consejo Directivo de la Organización Panamericana de la Salud. Esta participación enfatizó la importancia de integrar la acción climática en las prioridades del sector salud en las Américas, compartir lecciones de iniciativas regionales y fomentar la colaboración con los ministerios de salud y la OPS para acelerar sistemas de salud resilientes y de bajo carbono.

DescriptionFRE: Du 29 septembre au 3 octobre, Health Care Without Harm a participé à la 62e session du Conseil directeur de l'Organisation panaméricaine de la santé. Cette participation a souligné l'importance d'intégrer l'action climatique dans les priorités du secteur de la santé à travers les Amériques, de partager les leçons tirées des initiatives régionales et de favoriser la collaboration avec les ministères de la santé et l'OPS pour accélérer la mise en place de systèmes de santé résilients et à faibles émissions de carbone.
DescriptionPOR: De 29 de setembro a 3 de outubro, a Health Care Without Harm participou da 62ª Conferência Diretiva da Organização Pan-Americana da Saúde. Essa participação enfatizou a importância de incorporar a ação climática nas prioridades do setor de saúde em toda a América, compartilhando lições de iniciativas regionais e promovendo a colaboração com os ministérios da saúde e a OPAS para acelerar sistemas de saúde resilientes e de baixo carbono.

Title: NSA-2025-31-WPA_4
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 07: Health workforce;OCM 18: Social and environmental determinants;OCM 23: Health emergencies preparedness and risk reduction
HealthAgenda: Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services;Goal 3: Human Resources for Health;Goal 7: Evidence and Knowledge in Health;Goal 8: Outbreaks, Emergencies and Disasters
ProgressReport: Fernando Rementeria has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 11:33:04 AM: This session explored the dual challenge of scaling up health adaptation while advancing low-carbon operations, highlighting the synergies and trade-offs between adaptation and decarbonization. By the end, participants are expected to identify opportunities to integrate adaptation and mitigation in health systems, develop strategies that accelerate progress on both fronts, and outline implementation pathways that maximize benefits. The session emphasized the critical role of education and training programs for health professionals as essential infrastructure to strengthen climate resilience and support the transition to low-carbon operations. As a result, participants will be equipped to design approaches that not only manage risks but also build capacity, ensuring health systems can respond effectively to the climate crisis while reducing their environmental footprint.

ProgressReport: Fernando Rementeria has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 11:33:04 AM: HCWH has advanced conversations with the Ministry of Health of Costa Rica and PAHO’s country office, exploring alignment with ongoing initiatives of the Costa Rican Social Security Fund (CCSS) to quantify carbon emissions and strengthen climate resilience in health facilities. In Bolivia, discussions with PAHO representatives identified carbon emission reduction as a priority area; with a new administration since November, recommendations are being prepared to ensure that climate feature among its priorities, highlighting the value of applying HCWH’s Climate Impact Checkup tool to guide action. In El Salvador, authorities are exploring opportunities for climate action as a new ATACH member, aiming to link global commitments with national implementation. Parallel talks with the Andean Health Organization are framed under its strategic plan to build institutional capacity, developed with PAHO inputs and drawing on outcomes of the Smart Hospitals Initiative.

DescriptionENG: In 2025, Health Care Without Harm contacted national ministries of health throughout Latin America and PAHO country representatives to identify opportunities for collaboration on climate action within policy and planning frameworks related to climate and health. The outreach also included efforts to work with health facilities on quantifying carbon emissions and assessing climate vulnerability, conducted with PAHO’s technical guidance.
DescriptionSPA: En 2025, Health Care Without Harm se puso en contacto con los ministerios de salud nacionales de América Latina y los representantes de países de la OPS para mapear oportunidades de colaboración en la acción climática dentro de los marcos de políticas y planificación sobre clima y salud. El acercamiento también ha considerado el trabajo con las instalaciones de salud en la cuantificación de emisiones de carbono y evaluaciones de vulnerabilidad climática, realizadas con la orientación técnica de la OPS.

DescriptionFRE: En 2025, Health Care Without Harm a contacté les ministères de la santé nationaux à travers l'Amérique latine et les représentants des pays de l'OPS pour cartographier les opportunités de collaboration sur l'action climatique dans les cadres de politique et de planification concernant le climat et la santé. Cette démarche a également pris en compte le travail avec les établissements de santé sur la quantification des émissions de carbone et les évaluations de vulnérabilité climatique, réalisées avec l'orientation technique de l'OPS.
DescriptionPOR: Em 2025, a Health Care Without Harm entrou em contato com os ministérios da saúde nacionais em toda a América Latina e representantes da OPAS para mapear oportunidades de colaboração em ações climáticas dentro das estruturas de políticas e planejamento sobre clima e saúde. O contato também considerou o trabalho com instituições de saúde na quantificação de emissões de carbono e avaliações de vulnerabilidade climática, realizadas com a orientação técnica da OPAS.

Title: NSA-2025-31-WPA_6
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 07: Health workforce;OCM 18: Social and environmental determinants;OCM 23: Health emergencies preparedness and risk reduction
HealthAgenda: Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services;Goal 3: Human Resources for Health;Goal 7: Evidence and Knowledge in Health;Goal 8: Outbreaks, Emergencies and Disasters
ProgressReport: Fernando Rementeria has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 11:33:04 AM: In 2023, Health Care Without Harm opened access to the Climate Impact Checkup (CIC) tool, which has since generated growing demand and use in the region. Ministries of Health in at least 10 countries, including Argentina, Saint Lucia, Jamaica, Belize, and Guyana, have already applied the tool to measure and manage carbon emissions in health facilities. PAHO has also used the CIC as part of its Smart Hospitals initiatives, integrating the quantification of CO₂ emissions into its technical assistance. Complementing this, the CIC online course has been accessed across Latin America, and to date 500 professionals have been certified, strengthening regional capacity to calculate carbon footprints and embed this knowledge into health planning.

Esta herramienta ha permitido a la OPS aplicarla a través de múltiples iniciativas en América Latina y el Caribe, fortaleciendo la capacidad de la región para medir, gestionar y reducir las emisiones mientras avanza hacia sistemas de salud sostenibles y resilientes.

DescriptionFRE: L'organisation Health Care Without Harm a développé l'outil Climate Impact Checkup pour calculer les émissions de carbone dans les établissements de santé, complété par un cours en ligne. Cet outil a permis à l'OPS de l'appliquer à travers de multiples initiatives en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes, renforçant la capacité de la région à mesurer, gérer et réduire les émissions tout en faisant progresser des systèmes de santé durables et résilients.
DescriptionPOR: A Health Care Without Harm desenvolveu a ferramenta Climate Impact Checkup para calcular as emissões de carbono em instituições de saúde, complementada por um curso online. Essa ferramenta permitiu à OPAS aplicá-la por meio de várias iniciativas na América Latina e no Caribe, fortalecendo a capacidade da região de medir, gerenciar e reduzir emissões, ao mesmo tempo em que avança em sistemas de saúde sustentáveis e resilientes.

DescriptionENG: The Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure (CDRI), along with PAHO and Health Care Without Harm, is creating an online course on Resilient Health Infrastructure (RHI). As members of the Advisory Group for the course development, PAHO and HCWH provide expertise in climate, health, and sustainability, ensuring that the course meets regional needs and supports the development of stronger, climate-resilient health systems in Latin America and the Caribbean.
DescriptionSPA: La Coalición para la Infraestructura Resiliente ante Desastres (CDRI), junto con la OPS y Salud Sin Daño, está desarrollando un curso en línea sobre Infraestructura Sanitaria Resiliente (RHI). Como miembros del Grupo Asesor para el desarrollo del curso, la OPS y Salud Sin Daño aportan su experiencia en clima, salud y sostenibilidad, asegurando que el curso aborde las necesidades regionales y apoye sistemas de salud más fuertes y resilientes al clima en América Latina y el Caribe.

DescriptionFRE: La Coalition pour une infrastructure résiliente face aux catastrophes (CDRI), en collaboration avec l'OPS et Health Care Without Harm, développe un cours en ligne sur l'infrastructure de santé résiliente (RHI). En tant que membres du groupe consultatif pour le développement du cours, l'OPS et HCWH apportent leur expertise en matière de climat, de santé et de durabilité, garantissant que le cours répond aux besoins régionaux et soutient des systèmes de santé plus solides et résilients face au climat en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes.
DescriptionPOR: A Coalizão para Infraestrutura Resiliente a Desastres (CDRI), juntamente com a OPAS e a Saúde Sem Dano, está desenvolvendo um curso online sobre Infraestrutura de Saúde Resiliente (RHI). Como membros do Grupo Consultivo para o desenvolvimento do curso, a OPAS e a Saúde Sem Dano contribuem com expertise em clima, saúde e sustentabilidade, garantindo que o curso atenda às necessidades regionais e apoie sistemas de saúde mais fortes e resilientes às mudanças climáticas na América Latina e no Caribe.

DescriptionENG: The Mexico Declaration was published in January 2025, with the results of the Third Summit on Health of the Americas held on November 27 and 28, 2024, at the Chamber of Deputies in Mexico City. This event represented a space for dialogue and reflection on the challenges and opportunities in cardiovascular health in Latin America, from Mexico to Argentina.

Este evento representó un espacio para el diálogo y la reflexión sobre los desafíos y oportunidades en la salud cardiovascular en Latinoamérica desde México hasta Argentina.

ProgressReport: araceli.delgado has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 03:29:31 PM: Se participó en la sesión titulada “Abordando la multimorbilidad en el contexto de las enfermedades cardiovasculares” donde se discutieron los mecanismos e interacciones entre las enfermedades cardiovasculares (ECV) y otras afecciones crónicas, se revisaron enfoques basados en la evidencia para el manejo de la multimorbilidad en pacientes con ECV —centrándose en las sinergias terapéuticas y los posibles conflictos—, y se evaluó el impacto de la multimorbilidad en los resultados del tratamiento, la calidad de vida y la utilización de los servicios de salud en pacientes con ECV.

DescriptionENG:
Campaign Heart of Mom (#HeartOfMom)  
Messages and actions are shared on social media along with institutional videos to raise awareness among the population about cardiovascular diseases during pregnancy, with clear messages regarding this issue and simple, concrete measures to prevent them.
DescriptionSPA:
Campaña Corazón de Mamá (#CorazónDeMamá)  
Se comparten mensajes y acciones en redes sociales junto con videos institucionales para concientizar a la población sobre las enfermedades cardiovasculares en el embarazo, con mensajes claros sobre esta problemática y los cuidados simples y concretos para prevenirlas.

DescriptionFRE:
Campagne Cœur de Maman (#CœurDeMaman)  
Des messages et des actions sont partagés sur les réseaux sociaux, accompagnés de vidéos institutionnelles, pour sensibiliser la population aux maladies cardiovasculaires pendant la grossesse, avec des messages clairs sur ce problème et des soins simples et concrets pour les prévenir.
DescriptionPOR:
Campanha Coração de Mamãe (#CoraçãoDeMamãe)  
Mensagens e ações são compartilhadas nas redes sociais junto com vídeos institucionais para conscientizar a população sobre as doenças cardiovasculares na gravidez, com mensagens claras sobre essa problemática e cuidados simples e concretos para preveni-las.

ProgressReport: araceli.delgado has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 03:29:31 PM: El mes de septiembre #MueveTuCorazón y mejora tu salud cardiovascular Desde SIAC, se promovió la actividad física de las Américas, a través del posicionamiento de mensajes: La inactividad física es un factor de riesgo clave para padecer enfermedades cardiovasculares, cáncer y diabetes. Aquellas personas que realizan actividad física reducen entre un 20 y 30% sus probabilidades de morir prematuramente. - #Reto25×25. Consiste en realizar actividad física al menos 25 minutos al día durante 25 días continuos. - Webinar gratuito para la comunidad, destinado a abordar los aspectos fundamentales para mantener una salud cardiovascular óptima. Los 8 esenciales se trata de 8 puntos clave a seguir para prevenir las enfermedades cardiovasculares.

DescriptionENG: Celebration of World Heart Day. Every year on September 29, World Heart Day (WHD) is celebrated as a reminder for everyone around the world to take proper care of their hearts. This campaign is driven by the World Heart Federation (WHF). This year, the WHD slogan is "Don't Miss a Beat," encouraging the global community to adopt healthy habits and monitor their cardiovascular health.

DescriptionFRE:
Célébration de la Journée Mondiale du Cœur. Le 29 septembre de chaque année, on célèbre la Journée Mondiale du Cœur (JMC), un rappel pour que toutes les personnes à travers le monde prennent soin de leur cœur. Cette campagne est soutenue par la Fédération Mondiale du Cœur (FMC).  
Cette année, le slogan de la JMC est « Ne manquez pas un battement », incitant toute la communauté mondiale à adopter des habitudes saines et à surveiller leur santé cardiovasculaire.
DescriptionPOR:
Celebração do Dia Mundial do Coração. No dia 29 de setembro de cada ano, celebra-se o Dia Mundial do Coração (DMC), um lembrete para que todas as pessoas em todo o mundo cuidem adequadamente de seu coração. Esta campanha é promovida pela Federação Mundial do Coração (FMC).  
Este ano, o lema do DMC é “Não Perca um Batimento”, incentivando toda a comunidade global a adotar hábitos saudáveis e a monitorar sua saúde cardiovascular.

DescriptionENG: Speed Management Training for national and local decision-makers in Mexico. Participants included representatives from federal, state, and municipal government agencies, international organizations, civil society, academia, and multilateral agencies. PAHO was a key participant, represented by Vivian Pérez.

Los participantes incluyeron representantes de agencias gubernamentales federales, estatales y municipales, organizaciones internacionales, sociedad civil, academia y agencias multilaterales. La OPS fue un participante clave, representada por Vivian Pérez.

Title: NSA-2025-44-WPA_3
StrategicPlan: OCM 15: Intersectoral response to violence and injuries

ProgressReport: Alejandro Schwedhelm has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 03:48:05 PM: This emphasis built on PAHO’s contribution at the SENATRAN International Road Safety Seminar in December 2024 (where PAHO was invited by WRI). In that panel, Victor Pavarino highlighted the importance of incorporating national-level policy dialogue on motorcycle safety, a recommendation that helped shape the 2025 conference agenda. Subtitled “Protecting Lives on Two Wheels,” the event included several dedicated sessions on motorcycle safety policy. A key outcome was the presentation of a preliminary study by Vital Strategies on the safety impacts of motorcycle lanes, which showed inconclusive results and led SENATRAN to reconsider new approvals in the near term. This activity aligns with the Supporting National-Level Policies and Plans component of the current work plan, as it focuses on identifying national-level strategies. It also contributes to the Improving Road Safety for Vulnerable Users, and the Hosting and Participating in Webinars, Workshops, and Events components.

Title: NSA-2025-45-WPA_2
StrategicPlan: OCM 22: Research, ethics, and innovation for health;OCM 17: Elimination of communicable diseases;OCM 08: Access to health technologies;OCM 04: Response capacity for communicable diseases;OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 02: Health throughout the life course

ProgressReport: Laura Acebal has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:30:38 PM: In 2025, DNDi participated in the Chagas Coalition training plan, coordinating content on clinical care and immunosuppression. Under EMTCT Plus, capacity-building activities reached six sites in Colombia and one in Guatemala. DNDi co-organized a binational workshop between Colombia and Guatemala to improve demand for trypanocidal treatment and share lessons learned, with participation from Chagas Program of Guatemala and Colombia and PAHO strategic fund. In Guatemala, DNDi supported the development of an RDT verification protocol and donated ELISA kits. In Colombia, DNDi supported the INS in organizing expert workshops to define new recommendations for RDT use in national algorithms. DNDi joined the organizing committee for a national meeting of Chagas-affected persons in Bolivia, contributed to World Chagas Disease Day 2025, supported indicator analysis in Tarija, and engaged in subregional initiatives (INCOSUR, AMCHA, Central America) to strengthen national responses to Chagas.

Title: NSA-2025-45-WPA_3
StrategicPlan: OCM 22: Research, ethics, and innovation for health;OCM 17: Elimination of communicable diseases;OCM 08: Access to health technologies;OCM 04: Response capacity for communicable diseases;OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 02: Health throughout the life course

ProgressReport: Laura Acebal has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:30:38 PM: The findings from this study are pivotal in demonstrating the potential of shorter treatment durations: RDV+SOF 8-week regimen for people with HCV without cirrhosis is indicated for asymptomatic people who represent 80% of the HCV global burden. Malaysia MoH included this regimen into the national guidelines, and it will be recommended as 1st line RDV+SOF 8-week regimen is an excellent alternative for the programs in prisons (marginalized and underserved populations) For the primary health level, RDV+SOF 8 weeks allows treating more people with the same budget (8 weeks is cheaper than 12 weeks), more adherence (because of the shorter time getting pills) Furthermore, the RDV+SOF 8-week regimen may stimulate competition with other available Direct-Acting Antivirals (DAAs), potentially driving down costs and increasing accessibility

DescriptionENG: 3. Hepatitis: In the effort to promote affordable Hepatitis C treatment through generic competition, significant progress has been made in making Ravidasvir available as a viable treatment option. In March 2025, DNDi shared promising results from a study comparing the non-inferiority of Ravidasvir/Sofosbuvir (RDV/SOF) over 8 weeks versus 12 weeks for individuals with HCV without cirrhosis. This study was sponsored by the Malaysian Ministry of Health and received conditional approval from the Malaysian National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency (April 25).
DescriptionSPA: 3. Hepatitis: En la búsqueda de promover un tratamiento asequible para la Hepatitis C a través de la competencia de genéricos, se ha logrado un progreso significativo en la disponibilidad de Ravidasvir como una opción de tratamiento viable. En marzo de 2025, DNDi compartió resultados prometedores de un estudio que comparaba la no inferioridad de Ravidasvir/Sofosbuvir (RDV/SOF) durante 8 semanas frente a 12 semanas para individuos con HCV sin cirrosis. Este estudio fue patrocinado por el Ministerio de Salud de Malasia y recibió aprobación condicional de la NPRA de Malasia (Abr25).

DescriptionFRE: 3. Hépatite : Dans la quête de promouvoir un traitement abordable de l'hépatite C par la concurrence des génériques, des progrès significatifs ont été réalisés pour rendre le Ravidasvir disponible en tant qu'option de traitement viable. En mars 2025, DNDi a partagé des résultats prometteurs d'une étude comparant la non-infériorité du Ravidasvir/Sofosbuvir (RDV/SOF) sur 8 semaines par rapport à 12 semaines pour les individus atteints d'HCV sans cirrhose. Cette étude a été parrainée par le ministère de la Santé malaisien et a reçu une approbation conditionnelle de l'NPRA malaisien (25 avril).
DescriptionPOR: 3. Hepatite: Na busca por promover o tratamento acessível da Hepatite C por meio da concorrência de genéricos, progressos significativos foram feitos para tornar o Ravidasvir disponível como uma opção de tratamento viável. Em março de 2025, a DNDi compartilhou resultados promissores de um estudo que comparou a não inferioridade do Ravidasvir/Sofosbuvir (RDV/SOF) ao longo de 8 semanas em comparação com 12 semanas para indivíduos com HCV sem cirrose. Este estudo foi patrocinado pelo Ministério da Saúde da Malásia e recebeu aprovação condicional da NPRA da Malásia (Abr25).

Title: NSA-2025-45-WPA_4
StrategicPlan: OCM 22: Research, ethics, and innovation for health;OCM 17: Elimination of communicable diseases;OCM 08: Access to health technologies;OCM 04: Response capacity for communicable diseases;OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 02: Health throughout the life course
HealthAgenda: Goal 10: Communicable Diseases;Goal 7: Evidence and Knowledge in Health;Goal 5: Medicines, Vaccines and Technologies;Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services
ProgressReport: Laura Acebal has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:30:38 PM: In November 2025, PAHO and DNDi co-organized a high-level Drug Development Seminar in Brasília under the Dengue Alliance, bringing together Ministries of Health, PAHO, Fiocruz, SECTICS, and global south institutions. Sessions addressed the strategic value of dengue therapeutics, integration with vaccine and vector control strategies, ethical and regulatory aspects, digital surveillance, modeling, and diagnostics. The event reinforced the importance of coordinated R&D efforts and the leadership of the global south in tackling dengue. The agenda included presentations from Malaysia, India, Brazil, and regional stakeholders, emphasizing innovation pipelines, repurposing strategies, and barriers to implementation. PAHO’s participation underscored institutional support to align the development of dengue therapeutics with public health priorities.

DescriptionENG: 4. Dengue: DNDi supported scientific exchange and capacity-building for dengue management, focusing on research and development for new therapeutics. Activities include co-organizing expert dialogues and participating in the Dengue Alliance to promote South-South cooperation, strengthen the integrated response to arboviruses, and position dengue as a strategic priority for regional innovation and access.
DescriptionSPA: 4. Dengue: DNDi apoyó el intercambio científico y la capacitación para la gestión del dengue, con un enfoque en la I+D de nuevos tratamientos. Las actividades incluyen la coorganización de diálogos de expertos y la participación en la Alianza del Dengue para promover la cooperación Sur-Sur, fortalecer la respuesta integrada a los arbovirus y posicionar el dengue como una prioridad estratégica para la innovación y el acceso regional.

Title: NSA-2025-45-WPA_5

ProgressReport: Laura Acebal has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:30:38 PM: In leishmaniasis, PAHO and DNDi collaborated for the validation of the QUANTMET Leish tool with MoH of Brazil, Mexico, and Guatemala contributing to regional demand forecasting for leishmaniasis commodities. In Chagas, DNDi supported verification of RDTs in Guatemala, donated ELISA kits, and helped organize ETMI Plus trainings in Colombia and Guatemala. DNDi and PAHO supported a binational workshop and a national consultation in Colombia to update diagnostic protocols. DNDi also co-organized training and policy dialogues under the Chagas Coalition. For dengue, PAHO and DNDi co-organized a regional seminar in Brasília to discuss development and access to dengue therapeutics, engaging authorities, regulators, and researchers from the Global South.

Les activités conjointes comprenaient la prévision de la demande régionale, le soutien aux protocoles nationaux, le renforcement des capacités, l'accès aux diagnostics et aux traitements locaux, l'engagement politique et des discussions sur l'introduction de nouvelles technologies—renforçant les liens avec le Fonds stratégique de l'OPS et les soins de santé primaires intégrés.

As atividades conjuntas incluíram previsão de demanda regional, apoio a protocolos nacionais, capacitação, acesso a diagnósticos e tratamento local, engajamento em políticas e discussões sobre a introdução de novas tecnologias—fortalecendo os vínculos com o Fundo Estratégico da OPAS e a atenção primária integrada à saúde.

DescriptionENG: 6. Innovation (cross-cut/horizontal): DNDi initiated collaboration with PAHO’s Department of Innovation, Access to Medicines, and Health Technologies (IMT) to begin identifying concrete, phased activities that support regional innovation ecosystems and health technology production. Priority areas include regional governance of innovation, open science, upstream R&D capacity-building, and amplifying Latin America’s voice in global forums.
DescriptionSPA: 6. Innovación (transversal): DNDi inició una colaboración con el Departamento de Innovación, Acceso a Medicamentos y Tecnologías de la Salud (IMT) de la OPS para comenzar a identificar actividades concretas y por fases que apoyen los ecosistemas de innovación regional y la producción de tecnologías de la salud. Las áreas prioritarias incluyen la gobernanza regional de la innovación, la ciencia abierta, el fortalecimiento de capacidades en I+D en etapas tempranas y la amplificación de la voz de América Latina en foros globales.

DescriptionFRE: 6. Innovation (transversale/horizontale) : DNDi a initié une collaboration avec le Département de l'Innovation, de l'Accès aux Médicaments et des Technologies de la Santé (IMT) de l'OPS pour commencer à identifier des activités concrètes et phasées qui soutiennent les écosystèmes d'innovation régionaux et la production de technologies de la santé. Les domaines prioritaires incluent la gouvernance régionale de l'innovation, la science ouverte, le renforcement des capacités en R&D en amont, et l'amplification de la voix de l'Amérique latine dans les forums mondiaux.
DescriptionPOR: 6. Inovação (transversal/horizontal): A DNDi iniciou uma colaboração com o Departamento de Inovação, Acesso a Medicamentos e Tecnologias em Saúde (IMT) da OPAS para começar a identificar atividades concretas e faseadas que apoiem os ecossistemas de inovação regional e a produção de tecnologias em saúde. As áreas prioritárias incluem a governança regional da inovação, ciência aberta, capacitação em P&D upstream e ampliação da voz da América Latina em fóruns globais.

ProgressReport: secretariageneral has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:51:29 PM: ALIFAR avanzó en la elaboración de una propuesta para definir lineamientos generales y específicos de un proyecto orientado a fortalecer la producción y el acceso equitativo a medicamentos bioterapéuticos, incluidos biosimilares de calidad, seguros y eficaces, que será presentado a la OPS. El proyecto contemplará, entre otros aspectos, el análisis regulatorio y de financiamiento. ALIFAR presentará la propuesta a la OPS para su análisis y definición de próximos pasos. Además, ALIFAR ha colaborado con la OPS compartiendo la perspectiva de las empresas de América Latina en foros de discusión e intercambio de experiencias sobre el acceso a biosimilares en la región (Webinar, junio 2025). También compartió información sobre el caso argentino respecto del desarrollo y producción de productos bioterapéuticos similares.

ProgressReport: secretariageneral has provided the following results on 12/17/2025 04:51:29 PM: ALIFAR colaboró con la OPS en la conceptualización y desarrollo del programa de la XI Conferencia de la Red PARF. En línea con sus recomendaciones y en apoyo al Comité Directivo, aportó perspectivas e iniciativas para la renovación del plan de trabajo, incluyendo gobernanza y sostenibilidad. ALIFAR y la OPS firmaron un acuerdo de cooperación técnica con financiamiento para avanzar en armonización y convergencia regulatoria. Actualmente, se están realizando gestiones para completar el desembolso de los fondos y avanzar en la contribución técnica destinada a la actualización del material educativo del Itinerario Formativo REGTEC. En respuesta a solicitudes de la OPS, ALIFAR también contribuyó a la discusión de documentos sobre marcos regulatorios, avances regionales, comparaciones internacionales y propuestas de mejora, además de participar en la preparación y análisis de decisiones regulatorias y su impacto en el acceso oportuno a productos médicos.

DescriptionENG: In 2025, PAHO and CTFK conducted country missions, in coordination with other partners in the Bloomberg Initiative, to Brazil (May) and Mexico (April), focusing on tobacco tax initiatives in Brazil and overall strategic planning in Mexico. These missions provided essential technical assistance to local authorities and aimed to strengthen tobacco control efforts.
DescriptionSPA: En 2025, la OPS y CTFK llevaron a cabo misiones en los países, en coordinación con otros socios de la Iniciativa Bloomberg, a Brasil (mayo) y México (abril), con un enfoque en las iniciativas de impuestos al tabaco en Brasil y la planificación estratégica general en México. Estas misiones proporcionaron asistencia técnica vital a las autoridades locales y tuvieron como objetivo fortalecer los esfuerzos de control del tabaco.

DescriptionFRE: En 2025, l'OPS et le CTFK ont entrepris des missions dans les pays, en coordination avec d'autres partenaires de l'Initiative Bloomberg, au Brésil (mai) et au Mexique (avril), en mettant l'accent sur les initiatives fiscales sur le tabac au Brésil et la planification stratégique globale au Mexique. Ces missions ont fourni une assistance technique essentielle aux autorités locales et visaient à renforcer les efforts de lutte contre le tabagisme.
DescriptionPOR: Em 2025, a OPAS e a CTFK realizaram missões nos países, em coordenação com outros parceiros da Iniciativa Bloomberg, para o Brasil (maio) e o México (abril), com foco em iniciativas de tributação do tabaco no Brasil e no planejamento estratégico geral no México. Essas missões forneceram assistência técnica vital às autoridades locais e tiveram como objetivo fortalecer os esforços de controle do tabaco.

DescriptionENG: CTFK and PAHO/WHO collaborated closely with government ministries and civil society organizations across Brazil, Costa Rica, Mexico, Jamaica, Colombia, and Peru. Their advocacy for stronger tobacco control measures involved participation in public hearings, consultations, and providing technical input to legislative and executive branches.
DescriptionSPA: CTFK y la OPS/OMS colaboraron estrechamente con ministerios gubernamentales y organizaciones de la sociedad civil en Brasil, Costa Rica, México, Jamaica, Colombia y Perú. Su defensa de medidas más estrictas de control del tabaco involucró la participación en audiencias públicas, consultas y la provisión de insumos técnicos a las ramas legislativa y ejecutiva.

Leur plaidoyer en faveur de mesures de contrôle du tabac plus strictes a impliqué la participation à des audiences publiques, des consultations et la fourniture d'apports techniques aux branches législatives et exécutives.
DescriptionPOR: CTFK e a OPAS/OMS colaboraram de perto com ministérios governamentais e organizações da sociedade civil em todo o Brasil, Costa Rica, México, Jamaica, Colômbia e Peru. Sua defesa por medidas mais rigorosas de controle do tabaco envolveu a participação em audiências públicas, consultas e a prestação de contribuições técnicas aos poderes legislativo e executivo.

DescriptionENG: PAHO's comprehensive virtual course: training aimed at government, civil society organizations, and PAHO country and sub-regional staff to support and advance policies and regulatory measures designed to reduce the demand for and supply of sugar-sweetened beverages and energy-dense, nutrient-poor products. DescriptionSPA: Curso virtual integral de la OPS: capacitación dirigida a gobiernos, organizaciones de la sociedad civil y personal de la OPS a nivel nacional y subregional para apoyar y promover políticas y medidas regulatorias destinadas a reducir la demanda y la oferta de bebidas azucaradas y productos densos en energía y pobres en nutrientes. La difusión del curso fue apoyada por GHAI.

A disseminação do curso foi apoiada pelo GHAI.

DescriptionENG: Vanessa Mello Rodrigues, GHAI Food Policy Program In-country Coordinator in Brazil, joined a panel at the Pan American Health Organization/World Health Organization Regional Meeting on Noncommunicable Disease Prevention and Control on March 26th, 2025, held in Brasília, Brazil. GHAI focused on industry interference in policies to reduce the consumption of tobacco, alcohol, and ultra-processed foods.

GHAI se centró en la interferencia de la industria en las políticas para reducir el consumo de tabaco, alcohol y alimentos ultraprocesados.

DescriptionENG: PAHO and GHAI collaborated on multiple meetings and workshops to promote Front-of-Pack Warning Labels (FOPWL): PAHO participated in GHAI-supported education in the Caribbean by presenting on designing evidence-based FOPWL models. PAHO held the Sub-Regional Forum on Food Labeling Legislation in Central America, where GHAI participated and sponsored civil society involvement. GHAI also took part in PAHO’s Workshop on the Implementation of Front-of-Pack Labeling and Food and Beverage Marketing Regulations in Mexico City.
DescriptionSPA: La OPS y GHAI colaboraron en múltiples reuniones y talleres para promover el etiquetado frontal de advertencia de alimentos y bebidas (FOPWL, por sus siglas en inglés): la OPS participó en la educación apoyada por GHAI en el Caribe presentando sobre el diseño de modelos de FOPWL basados en evidencia. La OPS llevó a cabo el Foro Subregional sobre Legislación de Etiquetado de Alimentos en Centroamérica; GHAI participó y patrocinó la participación de la sociedad civil. GHAI participó en el Taller de la OPS sobre la Implementación del Etiquetado Frontal y las Regulaciones de Marketing de Alimentos y Bebidas en la Ciudad de México.

DescriptionFRE: L'OPS et le GHAI ont collaboré à plusieurs réunions et ateliers pour promouvoir le FOPWL : l'OPS a participé à l'éducation soutenue par le GHAI dans les Caraïbes en présentant des modèles de FOPWL basés sur des preuves. L'OPS a organisé le Forum sous-régional sur la législation en matière d'étiquetage des aliments en Amérique centrale ; le GHAI a participé et a sponsorisé la participation de la société civile. Le GHAI a également participé à l'atelier de l'OPS sur la mise en œuvre de l'étiquetage en façade des emballages et des réglementations sur le marketing des aliments et des boissons à Mexico.
DescriptionPOR: A OPAS e a GHAI colaboraram em múltiplos encontros e oficinas para promover o FOPWL: a OPAS participou da educação apoiada pela GHAI no Caribe, apresentando sobre o design de modelos de FOPWL baseados em evidências. A OPAS realizou o Fórum Sub-Regional sobre Legislação de Rotulagem de Alimentos na América Central; a GHAI participou e patrocinou a participação da sociedade civil. A GHAI participou da Oficina da OPAS sobre a Implementação da Rotulagem Frontal e das Regulamentações de Marketing de Alimentos e Bebidas na Cidade do México.

HealthAgenda: Goal 11: Health Inequalities and Inequities;Goal 3: Human Resources for Health;Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services;Goal 10: Communicable Diseases;Goal 5: Medicines, Vaccines and Technologies;Goal 7: Evidence and Knowledge in Health;Goal 8: Outbreaks, Emergencies and Disasters
ProgressReport: Bilal Khan has provided the following results on 12/18/2025 10:25:00 AM: Regional Engagement Series for Advancing Cervical Cancer Elimination in Latin America & the Caribbean (September–December 2025) To advance knowledge sharing and build momentum toward elimination, the Global HPV Consortium (GHC) launched a four-part virtual engagement series in 2025. Participants include Ministries of Health, researchers, civil society, advocacy and service delivery organizations from eight Latin American countries, alongside PAHO representatives and international implementers. Sessions & materials will be simultaneously interpreted across English, Spanish, and Portuguese. Developed through a co-creation process informed by surveys and key informant discussions, the series aims to share strategies, identify challenges, co-develop solutions, and strengthen regional networks. Expected outcomes include a stakeholder directory to enable collaboration, a compendium of best practices, and a practical toolkit of training and communication resources.

Title: NSA-2025-53-WPA_1
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 05: 5 Access to services for NCDs and mental health conditions
HealthAgenda: Goal 9: Noncommunicable Diseases;Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services
History: Nicole Kim has created a new workplan activity on 12/23/2025 11:36:24 AM
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
StrategicPlanENG: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services; OCM 05: Access to services for non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health conditions.
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 01: Acceso a servicios de salud integrales y de calidad; OCM 05: Acceso a servicios para enfermedades no transmisibles y condiciones de salud mental.
DescriptionENG: 1. Continued training and operational development for nurses, pharmacists, and oncology clinic staff: Ongoing practical and theoretical oncology training for nurses, pharmacists, and clinic staff, along with support for developing and strengthening clinical operations, in line with national and regional priorities. Activities include curriculum design, training delivery, and support for operational system improvements to enhance workflows, patient and staff safety, and the delivery of patient care.
DescriptionSPA:
1. Capacitación continua y desarrollo operativo para enfermeras, farmacéuticos y personal de clínicas de oncología:  
Capacitación práctica y didáctica continua en oncología para enfermeras, farmacéuticos y personal de la clínica, junto con apoyo para desarrollar y fortalecer las operaciones clínicas, alineadas con las prioridades nacionales y regionales. Las actividades incluyen el diseño de currículos, la entrega de capacitación y el apoyo a mejoras en los sistemas operativos para optimizar los flujos de trabajo, la seguridad de los pacientes y el personal, y la prestación de atención al paciente.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles de la santé mentale.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 05: Acesso a serviços para doenças não transmissíveis e condições de saúde mental.
DescriptionFRE:
1. Formation continue et développement opérationnel pour les infirmiers, pharmaciens et le personnel des cliniques d'oncologie :  
Formation pratique et didactique continue en oncologie pour les infirmiers, pharmaciens et le personnel des cliniques, ainsi que soutien au développement et au renforcement des opérations cliniques, en accord avec les priorités nationales et régionales. Les activités comprennent la conception de programmes, la livraison de formations et le soutien à l'amélioration des systèmes opérationnels pour optimiser les flux de travail, la sécurité des patients et du personnel, ainsi que la prestation des soins aux patients.
DescriptionPOR:
1. Treinamento contínuo e desenvolvimento operacional para enfermeiros, farmacêuticos e equipe de clínicas de oncologia:  
Treinamento prático e didático contínuo em oncologia para enfermeiros, farmacêuticos e equipe da clínica, juntamente com suporte para desenvolver e fortalecer as operações clínicas, alinhadas com as prioridades nacionais e regionais. As atividades incluem design de currículos, entrega de treinamentos e apoio a melhorias nos sistemas operacionais para aprimorar fluxos de trabalho, segurança de pacientes e funcionários, e a entrega de cuidados aos pacientes.

Title: NSA-2025-53-WPA_2
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 05: 5 Access to services for NCDs and mental health conditions
HealthAgenda: Goal 9: Noncommunicable Diseases;Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services
History: Nicole Kim has created a new workplan activity on 12/23/2025 11:36:24 AM
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
StrategicPlanENG: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services; OCM 05: Access to services for non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health conditions.
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 01: Acceso a servicios de salud integrales y de calidad; OCM 05: Acceso a servicios para enfermedades no transmisibles y condiciones de salud mental.
DescriptionENG:
2. Continued support for medical oncology through tumor boards and gynecologic oncology surgery:  
Expand specialist oncology support through regular multidisciplinary tumor boards and capacity building for gynecologic oncology surgery. Activities include sharing case-based knowledge, providing mentorship, and supporting decision-making for complex patient cases, and integrating best practices into national and regional cancer strategies in coordination with PAHO.
DescriptionSPA:
2. Apoyo continuo a la oncología médica a través de comités de tumores y cirugía de oncología ginecológica:  
Expandir el apoyo especializado en oncología mediante comités de tumores multidisciplinarios regulares y el fortalecimiento de capacidades para la cirugía de oncología ginecológica. Las actividades incluyen compartir conocimientos basados en casos, proporcionar mentoría y apoyar la toma de decisiones para casos complejos de pacientes, así como integrar las mejores prácticas en las estrategias nacionales y regionales de cáncer en coordinación con la OPS.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles de la santé mentale.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 05: Acesso a serviços para doenças não transmissíveis e condições de saúde mental.
DescriptionFRE:
2. Soutien continu à l'oncologie médicale par le biais de comités de tumeurs et de la chirurgie en oncologie gynécologique :  
Élargir le soutien spécialisé en oncologie grâce à des comités de tumeurs multidisciplinaires réguliers et au renforcement des capacités pour la chirurgie en oncologie gynécologique. Les activités comprennent le partage de connaissances basées sur des cas, la fourniture de mentorat, le soutien à la prise de décision pour des cas de patients complexes, et l'intégration des meilleures pratiques dans les stratégies nationales et régionales de lutte contre le cancer en coordination avec l'OPS.
DescriptionPOR:
2. Apoio contínuo à oncologia médica por meio de comitês de tumores e cirurgia oncológica ginecológica:  
Expandir o suporte especializado em oncologia por meio de comitês de tumores multidisciplinares regulares e capacitação para cirurgia oncológica ginecológica. As atividades incluem compartilhar conhecimentos baseados em casos, fornecer mentoria e apoiar a tomada de decisões para casos complexos de pacientes, além de integrar as melhores práticas nas estratégias nacionais e regionais de câncer em coordenação com a OPAS.

Title: NSA-2025-53-WPA_3
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 05: 5 Access to services for NCDs and mental health conditions
HealthAgenda: Goal 9: Noncommunicable Diseases;Goal 1: Equitable Access to Health Services
History: Nicole Kim has created a new workplan activity on 12/23/2025 11:36:24 AM
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
StrategicPlanENG: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services; OCM 05: Access to services for non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health conditions.
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 01: Acceso a servicios de salud integrales y de calidad; OCM 05: Acceso a servicios para enfermedades no transmisibles y condiciones de salud mental.
DescriptionENG:
3. Breast cancer prevention education through the development and distribution of an educational comic book:  
Develop, update, and distribute a culturally relevant breast cancer prevention comic book, with PAHO technical guidance, to promote early detection and health-promoting behaviors among communities in Belize.
DescriptionSPA:
3. Educación sobre la prevención del cáncer de mama a través del desarrollo y distribución de un cómic educativo:  
Desarrollar, actualizar y distribuir un cómic educativo sobre la prevención del cáncer de mama que sea culturalmente relevante, con la orientación técnica de la OPS, para promover la detección temprana y comportamientos que favorezcan la salud en las comunidades de Belice.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles de la santé mentale.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 05: Acesso a serviços para doenças não transmissíveis e condições de saúde mental.
DescriptionFRE:
3. Éducation à la prévention du cancer du sein par le développement et la distribution d'une bande dessinée éducative :  
Développer, mettre à jour et distribuer une bande dessinée sur la prévention du cancer du sein, culturellement pertinente, avec l'orientation technique de l'OPS, pour promouvoir la détection précoce et des comportements favorables à la santé au sein des communautés au Belize.
DescriptionPOR:
3. Educação sobre a prevenção do câncer de mama por meio do desenvolvimento e distribuição de uma história em quadrinhos educativa:  
Desenvolver, atualizar e distribuir uma história em quadrinhos sobre a prevenção do câncer de mama que seja culturalmente relevante, com orientação técnica da OPAS, para promover a detecção precoce e comportamentos saudáveis entre as comunidades em Belize.

Title: NSA-2025-53-WPA_4
StrategicPlan: OCM 01: Access to comprehensive and quality health services;OCM 05: 5 Access to services for NCDs and mental health conditions

StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 01: Acceso a servicios de salud integrales y de calidad; OCM 05: Acceso a servicios para enfermedades no transmisibles y condiciones de salud mental.
DescriptionENG:
4. Collaboration with the Belizean Ministry of Health & Wellness and Belize Cancer Society for breast and cervical cancer prevention outreach: 
Coordinate outreach campaigns and community education on breast and cervical cancer prevention and screening, such as a booth at the Pink Power Health Expo with the Belize Cancer Society.
DescriptionSPA: 4. Colaboración con el Ministerio de Salud y Bienestar de Belice y la Sociedad de Cáncer de Belice para la prevención del cáncer de mama y cervical: Coordinar campañas de divulgación y educación comunitaria sobre la prevención y detección del cáncer de mama y cervical, como un stand en la Expo de Salud Pink Power con la Sociedad de Cáncer de Belice.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles de la santé mentale.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 05: Acesso a serviços para doenças não transmissíveis e condições de saúde mental.
DescriptionFRE:
4. Collaboration avec le Ministère de la Santé et du Bien-être du Belize et la Société du Cancer du Belize pour la sensibilisation à la prévention du cancer du sein et du col de l'utérus : 
Coordonner des campagnes de sensibilisation et des programmes d'éducation communautaire sur la prévention et le dépistage du cancer du sein et du col de l'utérus, comme un stand lors de l'Expo Santé Pink Power avec la Société du Cancer du Belize.
DescriptionPOR: 4. Colaboração com o Ministério da Saúde e Bem-Estar de Belize e a Sociedade de Câncer de Belize para a prevenção do câncer de mama e colo do útero: Coordenar campanhas de conscientização e educação comunitária sobre a prevenção e triagem do câncer de mama e colo do útero, como um estande na Pink Power Health Expo com a Sociedade de Câncer de Belize.

StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 01: Acceso a servicios de salud integrales y de calidad; OCM 05: Acceso a servicios para enfermedades no transmisibles y condiciones de salud mental; OCM 16: Acción intersectorial en salud mental; OCM 18: Determinantes sociales y ambientales; OCM 21: Datos, información, conocimiento y evidencia; OCM 26: Temas transversales: Equidad, Etnicidad, Género y Derechos Humanos.
DescriptionENG: MOD and PAHO will work together to define classification criteria for infant mortality based on reducibility, designated as "preventable." PAHO will offer subject matter expertise along with other U.S.-based experts to refine a list of ICD-10 codes to estimate preventable infant deaths and contribute to reducing infant and under-five mortality.
DescriptionSPA: El MOD y la OPS colaborarán en la definición de criterios de clasificación para la mortalidad infantil basados en la reducibilidad y designados como "evitables". La OPS proporcionará experiencia en la materia junto con otros expertos de EE. UU. para refinar una lista de códigos ICD-10 que permita estimar la cantidad de muertes infantiles evitables y contribuir a la reducción de la mortalidad infantil y de menores de cinco años.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles mentaux ; OCM 16 : Action intersectorielle sur la santé mentale ; OCM 18 : Déterminants sociaux et environnementaux ; OCM 21 : Données, informations, connaissances et preuves ; OCM 26 : Thèmes transversaux : Équité, Ethnicité, Genre et Droits de l'homme.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 05: Acesso a serviços para doenças não transmissíveis e condições de saúde mental; OCM 16: Ação intersetorial em saúde mental; OCM 18: Determinantes sociais e ambientais; OCM 21: Dados, informações, conhecimento e evidências; OCM 26: Temas transversais: Equidade, Etnia, Gênero e Direitos Humanos.
DescriptionFRE: Le MOD et l'OPS collaboreront à la définition des critères de classification de la mortalité infantile en fonction de la réductibilité et désignée comme « évitable ». L'OPS fournira une expertise thématique ainsi que d'autres experts basés aux États-Unis pour affiner une liste de codes CIM-10 afin de déterminer une estimation des décès infantiles évitables et de contribuer à la réduction de la mortalité infantile et de la mortalité des enfants de moins de cinq ans.
DescriptionPOR: O MOD e a OPAS colaborarão na definição de critérios de classificação para a mortalidade infantil com base na reducibilidade e designados como "preveníveis". A OPAS fornecerá expertise no assunto, juntamente com outros especialistas baseados nos EUA, para refinar uma lista de códigos da CID-10 a fim de determinar uma estimativa de mortes infantis preveníveis e contribuir para a redução da mortalidade infantil e de crianças menores de cinco anos.

DescriptionENG: MOD launched a Low Dose Big Benefit Initiative aimed at increasing the use of low dose aspirin to prevent preeclampsia. In December 2025, a three-part webinar series began, in which PAHO, along with other international organizations, participated. PAHO provided the regional perspective on preeclampsia and the use of low dose aspirin. PAHO will contribute to ongoing discussions in webinars 2 and 3 in 2026.
DescriptionSPA: MOD lanzó una Iniciativa de Bajo Dosis y Gran Beneficio destinada a aumentar la utilización de aspirina en baja dosis para prevenir la preeclampsia. En diciembre de 2025, comenzó una serie de seminarios web en tres partes en la que participó la OPS junto con otras organizaciones internacionales. La OPS proporcionó la perspectiva regional sobre la preeclampsia y la utilización de LDA. La OPS contribuirá a las discusiones en curso sobre los seminarios web 2 y 3 en 2026.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 05 : Accès aux services pour les maladies non transmissibles et les troubles de la santé mentale ; OCM 16 : Action intersectorielle sur la santé mentale ; OCM 18 : Déterminants sociaux et environnementaux ; OCM 21 : Données, informations, connaissances et preuves ; OCM 26 : Thèmes transversaux : Équité, Ethnicité, Genre et Droits de l'homme.

DescriptionFRE: Le MOD a lancé une initiative intitulée "Bénéfice Élevé à Faible Dose" visant à augmenter l'utilisation de l'aspirine à faible dose pour prévenir la prééclampsie. En décembre 2025, une série de webinaires en trois parties a débuté, à laquelle l'OPS et d'autres organisations internationales ont participé. L'OPS a fourni une perspective régionale sur la prééclampsie et l'utilisation de l'aspirine à faible dose. L'OPS contribuera aux discussions en cours lors des webinaires 2 et 3 en 2026.
DescriptionPOR: A MOD lançou uma Iniciativa de Baixa Dose, Grande Benefício, com o objetivo de aumentar a utilização de aspirina em baixa dose para prevenir a pré-eclâmpsia. Em dezembro de 2025, começou uma série de webinars em três partes, na qual a OPAS, juntamente com outras organizações internacionais, participou. A OPAS forneceu a perspectiva regional sobre a pré-eclâmpsia e a utilização de LDA. A OPAS contribuirá para as discussões em andamento nos webinars 2 e 3 em 2026.

DescriptionENG: Within the framework of UNITE’s Policy Desk on Digital Health, initially focused on Mexico, UNITE and PAHO will collaborate to strengthen parliamentary engagement on digital health. PAHO will provide technical support to align discussions on sustainable financing and health data governance with regional strategies and national priorities, including strong legislation. This joint approach will be progressively extended to other countries in the region.

La OPS brindará apoyo técnico para alinear las discusiones sobre financiamiento sostenible y gobernanza de datos de salud con las estrategias regionales y las prioridades nacionales, incluyendo una legislación sólida. Este enfoque conjunto se extenderá progresivamente a otros países de la región.

DescriptionFRE: Dans le cadre du Bureau des politiques de la santé numérique d'UNITE, initialement axé sur le Mexique, UNITE et l'OPS collaboreront pour renforcer l'engagement parlementaire en matière de santé numérique. L'OPS fournira un soutien technique pour aligner les discussions sur le financement durable et la gouvernance des données de santé avec les stratégies régionales et les priorités nationales, y compris une législation solide. Cette approche conjointe sera progressivement étendue à d'autres pays de la région.
DescriptionPOR: No âmbito do Escritório de Políticas da UNITE sobre Saúde Digital, inicialmente focado no México, a UNITE e a OPAS colaborarão para fortalecer o engajamento parlamentar em saúde digital. A OPAS fornecerá suporte técnico para alinhar as discussões sobre financiamento sustentável e governança de dados de saúde com as estratégias regionais e prioridades nacionais, incluindo uma legislação robusta. Essa abordagem conjunta será progressivamente estendida a outros países da região.

DescriptionENG: Facilitate collaborative parliamentary involvement before and during high-level political forums, including UN High-Level Meetings, the World Health Assembly (WHA), COP, G7, G20, BRICS, and PAHO Governing Bodies, ensuring that parliamentarians are represented as key stakeholders. Through PAHO’s technical expertise and the facilitation of strategic engagement opportunities, UNITE and PAHO work together to enhance regional participation and elevate parliamentarians in regional and global health discussions.

A través de la experiencia técnica de la OPS y la facilitación de oportunidades de compromiso estratégico, UNITE y la OPS fortalecen conjuntamente la participación regional y colocan a los parlamentarios en la vanguardia de los diálogos sobre salud regional y global.

Grâce à l'expertise technique de l'OPS et à la facilitation d'opportunités d'engagement stratégique, UNITE et l'OPS renforcent conjointement la participation régionale et placent les parlementaires au cœur des dialogues régionaux et mondiaux sur la santé.
DescriptionPOR: Facilitar o engajamento parlamentar conjunto antes e durante fóruns políticos de alto nível, incluindo Reuniões de Alto Nível da ONU, Assembleia Mundial da Saúde (WHA), COP, G7, G20, BRICS e Órgãos de Governança da OPAS, garantindo que os parlamentares sejam representados como partes interessadas chave. Através da expertise técnica da OPAS e da facilitação de oportunidades de engajamento estratégico, a UNITE e a OPAS fortalecem conjuntamente a participação regional e colocam os parlamentares na vanguarda dos diálogos sobre saúde regionais e globais.

DescriptionENG: Facilitate joint parliamentary engagement ahead of the 2026 UN High-Level Meeting on Pandemic Prevention, Preparedness, and Response, and following the approval of the Pandemic Agreement at the 78th World Health Assembly. UNITE and PAHO will mobilize and engage parliamentarians through targeted activities to strengthen understanding of the agreement, support advocacy for its ratification, and explain the roles of WHO and PAHO in its implementation.
DescriptionSPA: Facilitar el compromiso parlamentario conjunto antes de la Reunión de Alto Nivel de la ONU sobre Prevención, Preparación y Respuesta a Pandemias de 2026, y tras la aprobación del Acuerdo sobre Pandemias en la 78ª Asamblea Mundial de la Salud. UNITE y la OPS movilizarán y involucrarán a los parlamentarios a través de actividades específicas para fortalecer la comprensión del acuerdo, apoyar la defensa de su ratificación y explicar los roles de la OMS y la OPS en su implementación.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 08 : Accès aux technologies de santé ; OCM 20 : Systèmes d'information intégrés pour la santé ; OCM 27 : Leadership et gouvernance.
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 01: Acesso a serviços de saúde abrangentes e de qualidade; OCM 08: Acesso a tecnologias de saúde; OCM 20: Sistemas de informação integrados para a saúde; OCM 27: Liderança e governança.
DescriptionFRE: Faciliter l'engagement parlementaire conjoint avant la Réunion de haut niveau des Nations Unies sur la prévention, la préparation et la réponse aux pandémies en 2026, et suite à l'approbation de l'Accord sur les pandémies lors de la 78e Assemblée mondiale de la santé. UNITE et l'OPS mobiliseront et impliqueront les parlementaires à travers des activités ciblées pour renforcer la compréhension de l'accord, soutenir le plaidoyer pour sa ratification et expliquer les rôles de l'OMS et de l'OPS dans sa mise en œuvre.
DescriptionPOR: Facilitar o engajamento parlamentar conjunto antes da Reunião de Alto Nível da ONU sobre Prevenção, Preparação e Resposta a Pandemias de 2026, e após a aprovação do Acordo sobre Pandemias na 78ª Assembleia Mundial da Saúde. A UNITE e a OPAS mobilizarão e envolverão parlamentares por meio de atividades direcionadas para fortalecer a compreensão do acordo, apoiar a defesa pela sua ratificação e explicar os papéis da OMS e da OPAS em sua implementação.

Description:

DescriptionENG: Co-organize a high-level regional parliamentary meeting, in collaboration with PAHO and the Inter-American Development Bank, bringing together parliamentarians, health finance experts, financial institutions, and development agencies to explore how political leadership can drive and sustain investments in health systems across the Americas. The discussion also aims to address sustainable financing for PAHO as a cornerstone of regional health resilience.
DescriptionSPA: Coorganizar un encuentro parlamentario regional de alto nivel, en colaboración con la OPS y el Banco Interamericano de Desarrollo, reuniendo a parlamentarios, expertos en financiamiento de la salud, instituciones financieras y agencias de desarrollo para explorar cómo el liderazgo político puede impulsar y sostener inversiones en los sistemas de salud en las Américas. La discusión también tiene como objetivo abordar el financiamiento sostenible para la OPS como un pilar de la resiliencia en salud regional.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 01 : Accès à des services de santé complets et de qualité ; OCM 08 : Accès aux technologies de santé ; OCM 20 : Systèmes d'information intégrés pour la santé ; OCM 27 : Leadership et gouvernance.

DescriptionFRE: Co-organiser un rassemblement parlementaire régional de haut niveau, en collaboration avec l'OPS et la Banque interaméricaine de développement, réunissant des parlementaires, des experts en financement de la santé, des institutions financières et des agences de développement pour explorer comment le leadership politique peut stimuler et soutenir les investissements dans les systèmes de santé à travers les Amériques. La discussion vise également à aborder le financement durable pour l'OPS en tant que pilier de la résilience sanitaire régionale.
DescriptionPOR: Co-organizar um encontro parlamentar regional de alto nível, em colaboração com a OPAS e o Banco Interamericano de Desenvolvimento, reunindo parlamentares, especialistas em financiamento da saúde, instituições financeiras e agências de desenvolvimento para explorar como a liderança política pode impulsionar e sustentar investimentos em sistemas de saúde nas Américas. A discussão também pretende abordar o financiamento sustentável para a OPAS como um pilar da resiliência da saúde regional.

StrategicPlanENG: OCM 08: Access to Health Technologies
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 08: Acceso a tecnologías de salud
DescriptionENG: USP will collaborate with PAHO to strengthen the regulatory competencies of professionals in Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC) through a structured learning approach aligned with PAHO’s REGTEC Learning Itinerary. This will include disseminating USP resources, including the USP LATAM education platform, which helps address identified gaps through WHO Global Benchmarking Tool (GBT) assessments and institutional development plans, as well as the organization and delivery of training sessions, workshops, and webinars.
DescriptionSPA: USP colaborará con la OPS para fortalecer las competencias regulatorias de los profesionales en América Latina y el Caribe (ALC) a través de un enfoque de aprendizaje estructurado alineado con el Itinerario de Aprendizaje REGTEC de la OPS. Esto incluirá la difusión de recursos de USP, incluida la plataforma de educación USP LATAM, que ayuda a abordar las brechas identificadas a través de las evaluaciones de la Herramienta de Comparación Global de la OMS (GBT) y los planes de desarrollo institucional, así como la organización y entrega de sesiones de capacitación, talleres y seminarios web.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 08 : Accès aux technologies de la santé
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 08: Acesso a tecnologias de saúde
DescriptionFRE: USP collaborera avec l'OPS pour renforcer les compétences réglementaires des professionnels en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes (ALC) grâce à une approche d'apprentissage structurée alignée sur le parcours d'apprentissage REGTEC de l'OPS. Cela inclura la diffusion des ressources de l'USP, y compris la plateforme éducative USP LATAM qui aide à combler les lacunes identifiées par les évaluations de l'outil de référence mondial de l'OMS (GBT) et les plans de développement institutionnel, ainsi que l'organisation et la réalisation de sessions de formation, d'ateliers et de webinaires.
DescriptionPOR: A USP colaborará com a OPAS para fortalecer as competências regulatórias de profissionais na América Latina e no Caribe (ALC) por meio de uma abordagem de aprendizado estruturada alinhada ao Itinerário de Aprendizagem REGTEC da OPAS. Isso incluirá a disseminação de recursos da USP, incluindo a plataforma de educação USP LATAM, que ajuda a abordar lacunas identificadas por meio de avaliações da Ferramenta de Benchmarking Global da OMS (GBT) e planos de desenvolvimento institucional, além da organização e entrega de sessões de treinamento, workshops e webinars.

Title: NSA-2025-27-WPA_2
StrategicPlan: OCM 08: Access to health technologies
HealthAgenda: Goal 5: Medicines, Vaccines and Technologies
History: Michael Schmitz has created a new workplan activity on 12/23/2025 12:04:57 PM
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
StrategicPlanENG: OCM 08: Access to Health Technologies
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 08: Acceso a tecnologías de salud
DescriptionENG: USP will collaborate with PAHO to enhance the capacity of Member States to prevent, detect, and respond to substandard and falsified (SF) medicines, including risks associated with impurities in active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), excipients, and finished products. This will involve facilitating access to technical tools and resources to improve risk assessment and verification processes. Activities will also leverage USP’s experience from the PQM/PQM+ programs, among others.
DescriptionSPA: USP colaborará con la OPS para fortalecer la capacidad de los Estados Miembros para prevenir, detectar y responder a medicamentos de calidad inferior y falsificados (SF), incluidos los riesgos relacionados con impurezas en los ingredientes farmacéuticos activos (API), excipientes y productos terminados. Esto incluirá facilitar el acceso a herramientas y recursos técnicos para mejorar los procesos de evaluación y verificación de riesgos. Las actividades también se basarán en la experiencia de USP en los programas PQM/PQM+, etc.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 08 : Accès aux technologies de la santé
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 08: Acesso a tecnologias de saúde
DescriptionFRE: USP collaborera avec l'OPS pour renforcer la capacité des États membres à prévenir, détecter et répondre aux médicaments de qualité inférieure et falsifiés (SF), y compris les risques liés aux impuretés dans les ingrédients pharmaceutiques actifs (API), les excipients et les produits finis. Cela inclura la facilitation de l'accès à des outils techniques et des ressources pour améliorer les processus d'évaluation des risques et de vérification. Les activités s'appuieront également sur l'expérience de l'USP tirée des programmes PQM/PQM+, etc.
DescriptionPOR: A USP colaborará com a OPAS para fortalecer a capacidade dos Estados Membros de prevenir, detectar e responder a medicamentos de qualidade inferior e falsificados (SF), incluindo os riscos relacionados a impurezas em ingredientes farmacêuticos ativos (APIs), excipientes e produtos acabados. Isso incluirá a facilitação do acesso a ferramentas e recursos técnicos para melhorar os processos de avaliação de risco e verificação. As atividades também se basearão na experiência da USP nos programas PQM/PQM+, etc.

Title: NSA-2025-27-WPA_3
StrategicPlan: OCM 08: Access to health technologies
HealthAgenda: Goal 5: Medicines, Vaccines and Technologies
History: Michael Schmitz has created a new workplan activity on 12/23/2025 12:04:57 PM
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
StrategicPlanENG: OCM 08: Access to Health Technologies
StrategicPlanSPA: OCM 08: Acceso a tecnologías de salud
DescriptionENG: USP will collaborate with PAHO to encourage the use of reference materials at the country and sub-regional levels. In this regard, USP will provide access to its educational courses, ensuring that regulators, laboratories, and manufacturers can utilize these resources to enhance quality assurance and build capacity. USP and PAHO will advocate for harmonized methods in the use of reference standards throughout Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC). These initiatives will prioritize Member States based on their regulatory maturity, income level, and public health requirements.
DescriptionSPA: USP trabajará con la OPS para promover el uso de materiales de referencia a nivel nacional y subregional. En consecuencia, USP facilitará el acceso a los cursos de educación de USP, asegurando que los reguladores, laboratorios y fabricantes puedan aprovechar estas herramientas para mejorar la garantía de calidad y desarrollar capacidades. USP y la OPS promoverán enfoques armonizados para el uso de estándares de referencia en toda la región de América Latina y el Caribe (ALC). Estas actividades priorizarán a los Estados Miembros en función del nivel de madurez regulatoria, el nivel de ingresos y las necesidades de salud pública.
StrategicPlanFRE: OCM 08 : Accès aux technologies de santé
StrategicPlanPOR: OCM 08: Acesso a tecnologias de saúde
DescriptionFRE: USP travaillera avec l'OPS pour promouvoir l'utilisation des matériaux de référence au niveau national et sous-régional. En conséquence, USP facilitera l'accès aux cours de formation USP, garantissant que les régulateurs, les laboratoires et les fabricants puissent tirer parti de ces outils pour améliorer l'assurance qualité et renforcer les capacités. USP et l'OPS promouvoir des approches harmonisées pour l'utilisation des normes de référence dans toute la région Amérique Latine et Caraïbes (LAC). Ces activités donneront la priorité aux États membres en fonction de leur niveau de maturité réglementaire, de leur niveau de revenu et de leurs besoins en santé publique.
DescriptionPOR: A USP trabalhará com a OPAS para promover o uso de materiais de referência em países e sub-regiões. Assim, a USP facilitará o acesso a cursos de educação da USP, garantindo que reguladores, laboratórios e fabricantes possam aproveitar essas ferramentas para melhorar a garantia da qualidade e desenvolver capacidades. A USP e a OPAS promoverão abordagens harmonizadas para o uso de padrões de referência em toda a América Latina e Caribe (ALC). Essas atividades priorizarão os Estados Membros com base no nível de maturidade regulatória, nível de renda e necessidades de saúde pública.

Title: NSA-2025-27-WPA_4
StrategicPlan: OCM 08: Access to health technologies
HealthAgenda: Goal 5: Medicines, Vaccines and Technologies

Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
